perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information may be disclosed only to employees or consultants of the Receiving Party who have a need to know.
perturbation.changed_text: Protected Data may be disclosed only to staff or advisors of the Receiving Party who have a need to know.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Protected Data,' and 'employees or consultants' is changed to 'staff or advisors.' While seemingly synonymous, 'Protected Data' might imply a higher level of security or a different subset of information than originally intended. 'Staff' and 'advisors' might have different legal responsibilities than 'employees' and 'consultants', which is an in-text contradiction because the term is still used elsewhere.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall remain the exclusiv e property of the Disclosing Party and nothing contained herein shall be construed as a grant, express or implied or by estoppel, of a transfer, assignment, license, lease of any right, title or interest in the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data' and 'employees or consultants' with 'staff or advisors'. The explanation states that 'Protected Data' might imply a different subset of information and 'staff' and 'advisors' might have different legal responsibilities. The contradicted text refers specifically to 'Confidential Information' and grants rights and interests based on that term. Because the changed text no longer uses the term 'Confidential Information' and the definitions of the new terms may be different, the unchanged contradicted text regarding 'Confidential Information' creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty regarding the obligations related to the data, as per the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will terminate two (2) years from the Effective Date, but may be terminated by either party at any time upon thi rty (30) days written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract will terminate two (2) years from the Effective Date, but may be terminated by either party at any time upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Agreement' is changed to 'Contract,' and the notice period is changed from thirty (30) days to ninety (90) days. The document elsewhere refers to the whole thing as an Agreement, thus the conflict is created. This is an in-text contradiction because the term is still used elsewhere as 'agreement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. Any amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be in writing and execut ed by a duly authorized representative of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Agreement' to 'Contract' while the contradicted text refers to the document as 'Agreement'. The perturbation explanation states this creates a contradiction because the document elsewhere refers to the whole thing as an Agreement. This violates the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, leading to an in-text contradiction because the terminology is used interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. Additionally, the change in notice period from 30 to 90 days could potentially impact other clauses related to termination, although without seeing those clauses directly, the primary contradiction lies in the terminology.

perturbation.original_text: If the Receiving Party becomes subject to a demand for discovery or disclosure of the Confidential Information of t he other party under legal process, such Receiving Party shall give to the other prompt notice of the demand prior to furnishing the Confidential Information demanded, and, at the expense of the Disclosing Party, shall obtain or cooperate with the Disclosi ng Party in seeking reasonable arrangements to protect the confidential and proprietary nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Receiving Party becomes subject to a request for production or dissemination of the Sensitive Data of the other party under subpoena, such Receiving Party shall give to the other immediate notification of the request prior to distributing the Sensitive Data requested, and at the sole cost of the Disclosing Party, shall obtain or collaborate with the Disclosing Party in seeking suitable resolutions to safeguard the secret and exclusive character of the Sensitive Data.
perturbation.explanation: Several terms are changed: 'demand for discovery' becomes 'request for production,' 'disclosure' becomes 'dissemination,' 'Confidential Information' becomes 'Sensitive Data,' 'legal process' becomes 'subpoena,' 'prompt notice' becomes 'immediate notification,' 'furnishing' becomes 'distributing,' 'expense' becomes 'cost,' 'reasonable arrangements' becomes 'suitable resolutions,' and 'confidential and proprietary nature' becomes 'secret and exclusive character.' These changes alter the legal tone, and it is an in-text contradiction, it creates ambiguity if 'Sensitive Data' equates to Confidential Information, with potentially conflicting legal implications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the completion or termination of any discussions between the parties, or at any time within fourteen (14) days of receipt of a written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall (i) promptly return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information disclosed in tangible form and copies thereof; or (ii) promptly destroy such Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) and certify their destruction to the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces new terms ('Sensitive Data,' 'request for production,' etc.) while modifying existing ones from the original text ('Confidential Information,' 'demand for discovery,' etc.). This creates a 'Misaligned Terminology' contradiction. The contradicted text refers specifically to 'Confidential Information.' If 'Sensitive Data' is not explicitly defined as equivalent to 'Confidential Information,' the Receiving Party could argue that the obligation to return or destroy 'Confidential Information' does not apply to 'Sensitive Data,' creating ambiguity and conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall remain the exclusiv e property of the Disclosing Party and nothing contained herein shall be construed as a grant, express or implied or by estoppel, of a transfer, assignment, license, lease of any right, title or interest in the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: All Proprietary Data shared under this agreement shall remain the sole holding of the Data Provider, and nothing contained herein shall be understood as an allocation, whether stated explicitly or implicitly, or through legal impediment, of a handover, allocation, authorization, rental, or charter of any entitlement, title, or stake in the Proprietary Data.
perturbation.explanation: The terminology changes are extensive: 'Confidential Information' becomes 'Proprietary Data,' 'Disclosing Party' becomes 'Data Provider,' 'exclusive property' becomes 'sole holding,' 'grant' becomes 'allocation,' 'transfer, assignment, license, lease' becomes 'handover, allocation, authorization, rental, charter,' and 'right, title or interest' becomes 'entitlement, title, or stake.' The changes are synonyms but not interchangeable, and create a contradiction, because it's unsure if 'Proprietary Data' has the same legal protection or obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in this Agreement, "Confidential Information” means any information, including without limitation, business information, technical, and marketing informati on, pricing, rates and discounts, provided such information is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or is disclosed in a manner that it may be reasonably inferred to be confidential and/or proprietary to the Disclosing Party. In the event of an inadvertent disclosure of source code, such information shall be immediately returned to the Disclosing Party. Confidential Information does not include information that: (i) becomes generally available to the public through no fault of the Receiving Party; (ii) is, prior to its initial disclosure hereunder, in the possession of the Receiving Party; (iii) is acquired by the Receiving Party from any third party without any restrictions on its use or disclosure; or (iv) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' and other terminology shifts creates a strong contradiction because the contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' with specific criteria and protections. By introducing a new term, the contract creates uncertainty about whether 'Proprietary Data' falls under the same definition and protections, or if it's governed by different rules. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, making it unclear which type of information is subject to the defined terms. This misaligned terminology, as explained, is a in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is valid only when signed by an employee with authority to bind that party. For YRC , this Agreement when unmodified may be signed by any Manager or higher in rank. However, if this Agreement is modified, it shall only be effective when accompanied by the YRC Legal Department’s signature or stamp of approval.
perturbation.changed_text: This Understanding is valid only when countersigned by an officer with agency to obligate that firm. In the case of YRC, this Understanding absent alterations may be signed by any Director or superior in position. However, should this Understanding be revised, it will solely be effective when accompanied by the YRC Legal Division’s inscription or seal of consent.
perturbation.explanation: There is a wholesale change in terminology, and it is an in-text contradiction since 'Agreement' becomes 'Understanding,' 'employee' becomes 'officer,' 'authority to bind' becomes 'agency to obligate,' 'party' becomes 'firm,' 'Manager' becomes 'Director,' 'higher in rank' becomes 'superior in position,' 'modified' becomes 'revised,' 'Legal Department' becomes 'Legal Division,' 'signature or stamp of approval' becomes 'inscription or seal of consent.' This creates ambiguity about who has the authority to sign and under what conditions, specifically concerning the YRC side of the agreement. Since the document refers to 'Agreement', it is unclear if this 'Understanding' has the same requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in this Agreement, "Confidential Information” means any information, including without limitation, business information, technical, and marketing informati on, pricing, rates and discounts, provided such information is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or is disclosed in a manner that it may be reasonably inferred to be confidential and/or proprietary to the Disclosing Party. In the event of an inadvertent disclosure of source code, such information shall be immediately returned to the Disclosing Party. Confidential Information does not include information that: (i) becomes generally available to the public through no fault of the Receiving Party; (ii) is, prior to its initial disclosure hereunder, in the possession of the Receiving Party; (iii) is acquired by the Receiving Party from any third party without any restrictions on its use or disclosure; or (iv) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a wholesale change in terminology ('Agreement' to 'Understanding', 'employee' to 'officer', etc.) as described in the Perturbation Explanation. This creates ambiguity about which version of the agreement the document is referring to and who has the authority to sign, specifically concerning YRC. The 'Contradicted Text' refers specifically to "this Agreement," and the change in terminology creates an inconsistency as to whether the requirements for 'Agreement' apply to the new 'Understanding'. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.
